title: Ornithopus glaucus
taxonomy: Ornithopus glaucus is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including beans, peas, lentils, and many more. Ornithopus glaucus is further classified within the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the most extensive subfamilies within the Fabaceae. The genus Ornithopus is a small one, containing only a few species of annual or biennial plants. Ornithopus glaucus is a specific species within this genus.

intro: Ornithopus glaucus, also known as White Bird's Foot Trefoil, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and distinctive white hairs. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Ornithopus glaucus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. White Bird's Foot Trefoil is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and other health conditions.
